title: Streaming orchestrator + TTFT/latency stats
description: internal/session package: owns a moonshine Stream, pulls audio chunks from the mic channel, calls add-audio + periodic transcribe_stream (respecting the library's built-in 200ms cache window, or MOONSHINE_FLAG_FORCE_UPDATE on an explicit poll interval), and computes/exposes: TTFT (time from stream start to first non-empty transcript line), per-poll latency (last_transcription_latency_ms from the API), total elapsed, words/sec. Emits updates over a channel for the TUI to consume.
close_reason: internal/session/session.go: Live orchestrator owning a moonshine.Stream, feeding mic chunks, polling on a ticker, computing TTFT (time to first non-empty line) + per-poll latency + elapsed, emitting Update structs on a channel. Verified end-to-end via 'moonshine live --no-tui': clean startup, listening loop, graceful SIGINT shutdown with correct final stats line (ttft=- elapsed=6.88s when no speech present, as expected).

title: P5b [Track B]: Gemini agent with function-calling (gemini.go)
description:
TRACK B. Owns internal/serve/gemini.go ONLY. The built-in LLM agent using google.golang.org/genai (Google Gemini) with native function-calling.

Build:
- GeminiAgent implements AgentHandler (P5a). On a finalized utterance, calls Gemini with a system prompt + function declarations:
    * lookup(query) -> calls Retriever (P5d), returns results to the model
    * display_card(title, body, kind) -> emits event.ActionRequest{Verb:display}
    * speak(text) -> event.ActionRequest{Verb:speak}
    * run_command(name, args) -> event.ActionRequest{Verb:run_command} (gated; off by default)
- Runs the function-calling loop: model -> tool call -> execute (lookup runs in-process; display/speak/run_command become ActionRequests) -> feed results back -> final response -> speak/display it.
- Config: --gemini-model, API key via env (GEMINI_API_KEY / GOOGLE_API_KEY). Model + client injected via an LLMClient interface so tests use a fake (no network).

TESTS: gemini_test.go with a FAKE genai/LLMClient — assert a scripted tool call maps to the right ActionRequest, lookup wires to a fake Retriever, and the loop terminates. NO real network / NO native libs in tests.
design: Define type LLMClient interface abstracting the genai call (GenerateWithTools(ctx, history, tools) -> (text, []toolCall)). Real impl wraps google.golang.org/genai; fake impl for tests. Add google.golang.org/genai to go.mod. Isolated to gemini.go; depends on P5a (AgentHandler) + P5d (Retriever) + P2 (Dispatcher/action verbs) + P1 (event types).
acceptance_criteria:
- GeminiAgent implements AgentHandler; function declarations for lookup/display_card/speak/run_command.
- Tool-call -> ActionRequest mapping + lookup->Retriever wiring + loop termination tested with a fake LLMClient (no network).
- go.mod has google.golang.org/genai.
- run_command gated off by default.
- No native libs in tests.
close_reason: Implemented GeminiAgent with google.golang.org/genai, native function-calling for lookup/display_card/speak/run_command, gating on run_command, and unit tests using fake LLMClient.

title: P3 [shared]: Transport interface (transport.go)
description:
Owns internal/serve/transport.go ONLY. Small, fast; unblocks both transport implementations so Track A can parallelize WS and gRPC.

Define:
  type Transport interface {
    Start(ctx) error
    Publish(event any) error          // fan-out serialized TranscriptEvent/DisplayCard
    Actions() <-chan event.ActionRequest
    Close() error
  }
Plus a Manager that runs multiple transports concurrently (--transport ws,grpc), merges their Actions() channels into one, and Publishes to all. Wire Manager.Actions() -> Dispatcher (P2) and Hub (P1) -> Manager.Publish in the eventual serve.go (P5).

TESTS: transport_test.go with a fake Transport verifying Manager merges Actions from 2 fakes and Publishes to both.
design: Keep interface minimal and JSON-oriented so WS (gorilla/nhooyr) and gRPC both satisfy it. Manager owns lifecycle + channel merge; individual transports stay dumb.
acceptance_criteria:
- Transport interface + Manager defined; Manager merge/publish unit tested with fakes.
- No native libs in tests.
close_reason: Merged in PR #4 (pending CI): Transport interface + Manager (multi-transport fan-out, action merge, partial-start-failure tolerance). Unblocks P4a (WebSocket) and P4b (gRPC) to run fully in parallel.

title: P2 [shared]: Dispatcher + TTS-back + barge-in (dispatcher.go, tts.go)
description:
Owns internal/serve/dispatcher.go and internal/serve/tts.go. Depends on P1 event types. Needed by both tracks (transports call Dispatcher; agents emit Actions through it).

Build:
1. dispatcher.go — Dispatcher.Handle(ActionRequest) ActionResult, routing verbs:
   - speak: args {text, voice?, speed?} -> TTS (calls tts.go), gated by allow-actions.
   - display: args = DisplayCard -> publish as event via Hub (inject Hub or a publish func).
   - session.pause/resume/stop: toggle mic feeding / cancel session ctx (inject control hooks).
   - agent.result: subscriber returning tool output -> optionally speak/display.
   - Unknown verb -> ActionResult{OK:false}.
2. tts.go — lazy moonshine.Synthesizer (internal/moonshine/tts.go:45) constructed on first speak, cached; Speak(text, opts) synthesizes and plays via audio.PlayFloat32 (internal/audio/playback.go). BARGE-IN GUARD: expose a Speaking() gate / mic-mute hook so the session stops feeding mic audio to the stream while TTS plays (prevents self-transcription). Document this is mute-based, not AEC.

TESTS: dispatcher_test.go routes each verb to a fake sink; unknown verb handled; allow-actions gating enforced. tts.go's synth/playback is native — keep it behind an interface so dispatcher_test uses a fake TTS (no native libs in tests).
design: Define interfaces so tests avoid native deps: type Speaker interface { Speak(ctx, text string, opts) error; Speaking() bool }. Dispatcher takes a Speaker, a Publisher (Hub), and SessionControl hooks — all injectable/fakeable. Real tts.go implements Speaker with Synthesizer+PlayFloat32.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dispatcher routes all verbs; unknown verb returns OK:false; gating respected (unit tested with fakes).
- Speaker interface + real TTS impl; barge-in mute hook exposed.
- 'go test ./internal/serve/...' passes, no native libs.
close_reason: Merged in PR #3 (pending CI): Dispatcher (verb routing, RegisterVerb extension point) + TTSSpeaker (lazy synth, barge-in Speaking() gate). Fixed a nil-interface-method panic in handleSessionControl during dev; full test suite + race + vet + gofmt clean.

title: P1 [shared]: event model + Hub foundation (internal/serve/event, hub.go)
description:
SHARED FOUNDATION — must land before Track A or Track B start. Owns internal/serve/event/event.go and internal/serve/hub.go. Freezes the event/action types both tracks depend on.

Build:
1. internal/serve/event/event.go:
   - TranscriptEvent: derived from session.Update. Fields: Lines (interim+final with ID, Text, StartTime, Duration, IsComplete, SpeakerLabel, optional Words), FinalizedLineIDs, TTFTms, ElapsedMs, PollLatencyMs, Done, Err. Reuse the JSON shapes already used by transcribe.go (transcribeResult) and teeUpdatesToFile for consistency.
   - ActionRequest{Verb string, Args json.RawMessage, ID string}; verbs: speak, display, session.pause, session.resume, session.stop, agent.result.
   - ActionResult{ID, OK bool, Err string}.
   - DisplayCard{Title, Body, Kind, Data} (fanned out to subscribers as an event).
   - FromUpdate(session.Update) TranscriptEvent converter.
2. internal/serve/hub.go:
   - Hub: Subscribe() returns a per-subscriber buffered channel; Publish(event) fans out; drop-oldest on slow subscriber (mirror session.send semantics, session.go:252). Unsubscribe/Close. Track finalized Line.IDs so a subscriber that dropped interim frames still gets each finalized line exactly once (idempotency helper usable by transports).
   - Ingest(<-chan session.Update): pump loop converting Updates to events and publishing.

TESTS (no native libs): hub_test.go with a fake update source — verify fan-out to multiple subscribers, drop-oldest under backpressure, and that every finalized Line.ID is delivered exactly once even when interim frames are dropped. event_test.go: round-trip (de)serialization.
design: This task freezes the public JSON contract. Do NOT change event/action struct field names after this lands without coordinating — both tracks serialize against them. Keep event package dependency-light (only imports internal/session + internal/moonshine types; no transport/agent imports) so it stays a leaf.
acceptance_criteria:
- internal/serve/event compiles; TranscriptEvent, ActionRequest, ActionResult, DisplayCard defined and JSON-tagged.
- FromUpdate maps session.Update fields correctly (unit tested).
- Hub fan-out, drop-oldest, and finalized-once semantics unit tested with fakes.
- 'go test ./internal/serve/...' passes with no native libs.

title: Epic: moonshine serve — agentic voice sidecar daemon
description:
Add a long-running 'moonshine serve' command that turns the existing STT+TTS pipeline into an agentic voice sidecar: streams live transcript events as JSON over IPC (WebSocket + gRPC) to any-language subscribers, and accepts inbound actions so a built-in Gemini agent (or an external subscriber) can react to finalized utterances — lookups/RAG, display push, voice commands, and speak-back via TTS.

ARCHITECTURE (event-driven, transport-agnostic):
  mic -> session.Live --Update--> Hub --event(JSON)--> subscribers (WS + gRPC)
                          ^                                   |
       action(JSON) ------+<----------------------------------+
                          |
                   Dispatcher --> Gemini agent (function-calling)
                          |            tools: lookup/retrieve, display_card, run_command, speak
                          +-- TTS (Synthesizer -> PlayFloat32, mic-mute barge-in guard)
                          +-- session control (pause/resume/stop)

KEY DECISIONS (locked):
- Keep pipeline in internal/; extension surface is IPC/JSON only (no pkg/ promotion).
- Daemon lives in a new internal/serve package + cmd/moonshine/serve.go.
- Transports: define a Transport interface first, then implement BOTH WebSocket and gRPC behind it (selectable/concurrent via --transport).
- LLM: Google Gemini via google.golang.org/genai, native function-calling.
- Agent actions: lookups/RAG, display push, voice commands, speak-back (TTS), LLM/tool-calling.

INTEGRATION POINTS IN EXISTING CODE:
- session.Live emits session.Update on Updates() channel (internal/session/session.go:156); serve is a 4th consumer alongside TUI/plain/file-tee (cmd/moonshine/live.go:119-135). Reuse dedup pattern from teeUpdatesToFile (live.go:144).
- Update distinguishes interim vs final via Line.IsComplete, stable Line.ID, and FinalizedLines per poll (session.go:33) — agent triggers on FinalizedLines only.
- session.send drops updates under backpressure (session.go:252); subscribers MUST be idempotent on Line.ID+IsComplete, never assume every frame is seen.
- TTS: moonshine.Synthesizer (internal/moonshine/tts.go:45) -> audio.PlayFloat32 (internal/audio/playback.go).
- Command wiring: rootCmd.AddCommand in cmd/moonshine/root.go:66-72; reuse live's --arch/--language/--providers + diarization flags.

CONSTRAINTS:
- internal/moonshine + internal/audio purego/cgo split must be preserved (AGENTS.md); mic is the only cgo exception.
- 'make test' must stay native-free: all Hub/Dispatcher/agent/transport logic testable with fakes (fake genai client, fake transport, fake session update source).
- Security: --allow-actions gates speak/session-control/run_command; run_command off by default.

PARALLELISM: work is split into Track A (transport/plumbing) and Track B (agent/actions) sharing a Phase-1 foundation. See child tasks; dependency edges enforce ordering so two agents don't collide.
design:
Package layout to avoid file collisions between two agents:
  internal/serve/event/     (Phase 1 — shared foundation, no collisions after done)
    event.go        TranscriptEvent, ActionRequest, ActionResult, DisplayCard, (de)serialization
  internal/serve/
    hub.go          Hub: fan-out session.Update -> N subscribers, drop-oldest, finalized dedup
    dispatcher.go   Dispatcher: route ActionRequest verbs (speak/display/session.*/agent.result)
    tts.go          lazy Synthesizer + PlayFloat32 + mic-mute barge-in guard
    transport.go    Transport interface (Publish/Actions/lifecycle)
    ws.go           WebSocket transport (Track A)
    grpc.go         gRPC transport (Track A)
    serve.proto     gRPC service + messages (Track A)
    agent.go        AgentHandler interface + ExternalAgent (Track B)
    gemini.go       GeminiAgent (google.golang.org/genai function-calling) (Track B)
    intent.go       fast-path regex/rules intent matcher (Track B)
    retriever.go    Retriever interface for RAG/lookup tool (Track B)
  cmd/moonshine/serve.go   serveCmd wiring (Phase 5, after both tracks land)

Each file is owned by exactly one task to keep two agents in separate files. Shared types (event package) are frozen in Phase 1 before either track starts.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'moonshine serve' starts, opens mic, and streams TranscriptEvent JSON to connected WS and gRPC subscribers with interim + finalized lines.
- A subscriber can POST an action (speak/display/session control) and observe the effect (TTS plays; display card fanned out; session pauses).
- Built-in Gemini agent, when enabled, turns a finalized utterance into a function call that maps to an action (lookup/display/speak) and speaks/display the result.
- Fast-path intent matcher handles at least one deterministic voice command without calling Gemini.
- Slow-subscriber backpressure drops interim frames but never loses a finalized line (idempotent on Line.ID).
- mic is muted during TTS playback (no self-transcription).
- --allow-actions gates speak/session-control/run_command; run_command off by default.
- 'make test' passes with no native libs (all new logic covered by fakes).

title: Epic: pkg/serveapi — public Go extension surface for Tier 2 agents
description: Add a public pkg/serveapi package so Go programs can implement AgentHandler/Retriever/LLMClient and link them in-process (Tier 2), instead of the currently-documented-but-broken 'import internal/serve' path (forbidden by Go internal/ rules for external modules). IPC/JSON (WS+gRPC) stays the primary, language-agnostic surface; pkg/serveapi is an ADDITIONAL Go-native on-ramp. Amends the locked decision in docs/serve-sidecar.md §2 ('no promotion to pkg/'). HARD CONSTRAINT: pkg/serveapi must build with CGO_ENABLED=0 — it must be a zero-internal-import leaf (stdlib + at most internal/moonshine, which is proven cgo-free; NEVER internal/session or internal/audio, which require cgo via malgo). Data/wire types are shadow structs (not type aliases) for stability. See docs/vision/serveapi-design.md for full rationale.
design: pkg/serveapi is a leaf package: interfaces (AgentHandler, CompositeHandler, AgentRunner, ExternalAgent, ActionSink; Retriever+impls; LLMClient interface only; AudioSource) + shadow structs (Line, Word, SpeakerSpan, TranscriptEvent, ActionRequest, ActionResult, DisplayCard, SpeakArgs, Kind consts). internal/serve becomes the consumer/source-of-truth-holder is inverted: pkg/serveapi holds the types, internal/serve imports them + holds conversion mappers (session.Update->TranscriptEvent) and concrete impls (GeminiAgent/genai, IntentMatcher, transports, Hub, Dispatcher, TTSSpeaker) that stay internal. 517 (swappable Transcriber) stays out — still design-stage.
acceptance_criteria: pkg/serveapi exists; CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./pkg/serveapi/... passes (CI-enforced); imports never include internal/session or internal/audio; internal/serve consumes pkg/serveapi with no duplicated type defs and existing tests green; serve-sidecar.md §2 amended; quickstart Tier 2 imports pkg/serveapi.

title: Per-connection session manager + resource limits

title: Remote-PCM AudioSource: accept client audio over WS/gRPC

design: Reuse WS/gRPC transports for an inbound audio stream; push decoded float32 chunks onto the AudioSource channel. Backpressure-aware.

Provide a mic-backed implementation preserving current behavior. Foundation for remote audio and file/stream transcription.
design: Interface in internal/audio or internal/session; MicCapture satisfies it. session.Live.Run feeds source chunks into Stream.AddAudio unchanged. Native-free tests with a fake source emitting canned PCM.
acceptance_criteria: session.NewLive takes an AudioSource; mic path behaves identically; fake-source unit test passes; go test ./... native-free.

title: Epic: Hostable cascade — run 'moonshine serve' beyond the local box
description: Enable hosting moonshine serve for remote clients (serve-in-a-box and bring-your-own-cloud), while keeping local-first as the default. The serve transport layer (Hub + WS/gRPC + JSON/proto) is already server-shaped and the STT engine is stateless PCM-in (Stream.AddAudio). Three edges assume a single local box and must be decoupled: (1) audio-in is hard-wired to audio.StartMicCapture and session.NewLive takes concrete *audio.MicCapture; (2) audio-out uses audio.PlayFloat32 (local device) + mic.SetMutedFunc barge-in; (3) one process = one mic = one broadcast Hub (no per-connection sessions). Deployment shapes: serve-in-a-box (container, demos/edge), BYO-cloud/self-host (recommended hosted shape, customer's VPC, privacy-preserving). Multi-tenant SaaS is intentionally left as an exercise for downstream users (not operated by this project, not architecturally forbidden). Thesis-preserving: audio stays where the operator puts it. See docs/hosting.md. Builds on the merged serve feature (epic moonshine-go-6nb).
design: One 'hostable cascade' core; deployment shapes differ by packaging/hardening only. Enabling refactors are edge-only; the model loop is untouched. AudioSource interface (mic | remote-PCM) also unblocks file/stream STT. Native-free tests via fake AudioSource where logic allows.
acceptance_criteria: moonshine serve can accept a remote client's audio (no local mic), return synthesized TTS audio over the transport, and run multiple independent sessions with resource limits; a container image runs it mic-less; BYO-cloud deploy artifacts + hosting docs exist; local-first default behavior is unchanged.

title: Surface confidence scores in TranscriptEvent for fast/slow routing
description: Lesson from online STT: confidence scores enable fast-path/slow-path routing (high confidence -> deterministic IntentMatcher; low confidence -> ask-to-repeat or escalate to LLM). Word.Confidence already exists in the binding (internal/moonshine/stt.go) and the C API (moonshine-c-api.h: 'Model confidence score, 0.0..1.0'). Surface per-word confidence (and/or an aggregated per-line score) through the serve event model (TranscriptEvent/Line) so agents and subscribers can route on it.
design: NO upstream caveat: confidence is already available at word level in Word.Confidence and in the C API. Work is: (a) ensure it flows into TranscriptEvent, (b) optionally compute a line-level aggregate (min/mean of word confidences) since Line has no confidence field, (c) document routing patterns. Frozen event types (serve-sidecar.md §8) may need a field add — coordinate if P1 already landed.
acceptance_criteria: TranscriptEvent carries word-level confidence (and optionally a line-level aggregate); a documented fast/slow routing example; native-free tests; no change to air-gapped guarantees.

title: Tunable endpointing/finalization policy for serve sessions
description: Lesson from Deepgram: a voice agent's responsiveness is dominated by ENDPOINTING (when an utterance is declared final), not raw WER. Today finalization is driven by moonshine's internal ~200ms re-analysis window and its built-in VAD (session.go trackLines / FinalizedLines). Add a configurable finalization policy in the session/serve layer: silence-duration threshold, min/max utterance length, and how long to wait after IsComplete before triggering the agent. This makes agent trigger latency a product knob instead of an implementation detail.
design: PARTIAL upstream caveat: moonshine-c-api.h exposes internal VAD with guarantees (lines never removed) but NO tunable endpointing/silence-threshold option in its recognized-options list. Basic policy (debounce/min-max utterance length, post-final delay) is implementable purely in moonshine-go's session/serve layer without upstream changes. Deeper VAD threshold tuning would require upstream libmoonshine support — VERIFY before promising that tier.
acceptance_criteria: serve exposes at least a basic, configurable finalization/endpointing policy (silence/debounce + min/max utterance) in the session layer; documented; native-free unit tests with a fake update source; upstream-VAD-tuning tier explicitly scoped as separate/blocked-on-upstream.

title: Docs: stt-landscape.md — what other cascade players (Deepgram/ElevenLabs/Whisper) teach us
description: Standalone landscape doc (docs/stt-landscape.md): what the online/other STT cascade-revival players do well and what moonshine-go's cascade can learn, even though they're cloud and we're local-first. Framing: 'local is a deployment property, not a capability'. Covers Deepgram (endpointing/VAD as tunable, keyterm boosting, formatting layers), ElevenLabs Scribe v2 (diarization/speaker labeling, word-timestamps + audio-event tagging, agent-positioned realtime), Whisper (multilingual/noise-robustness yardstick; chunk-batch not streaming-native = Moonshine's structural edge). Cross-cutting lessons: endpointing-is-the-product, structured transcripts, domain-vocab injection, confidence-driven fast/slow routing, hybrid end-state. Where online STT can't follow us (raw-audio egress, per-minute cost, can't co-locate STT+LLM+TTS). Hybrid future: swappable Transcriber backend framed as deliberate-but-uncommitted option mirroring swappable LLMClient, air-gapped-safe. Links each actionable lesson to its bd task. Companion to opportunities.md §7 (vs Live models) and serve-sidecar.md.
design: Prose-only, no build impact. All capability claims verified against internal/moonshine/stt.go and the moonshine-c-api.h header: Word.Confidence + model confidence score EXIST (no caveat); identify_speakers/word_timestamps/diarization_* options EXIST; keyterm/keyword/vocab boosting does NOT exist upstream (genuine caveat); no tunable endpointing/VAD option upstream (basic finalization policy implementable in session layer, deeper VAD needs upstream). Competitor claims stated factually. No invented moonshine flags.
acceptance_criteria: docs/stt-landscape.md exists; per-vendor strengths (Deepgram/ElevenLabs/Whisper) + cross-cutting lessons + 'where they can't follow' + hybrid-future section; each actionable lesson links its bd task; caveats accurate to the C API header; cross-linked from docs/README.md, README.md, and opportunities.md.

Each lesson links its bd task (qii/kpd/7kr/517). Cross-linked from docs/README.md, README.md, opportunities.md §7.

Tier 0 (subscribe, any language): connect WS, print TranscriptEvent JSON, honor idempotency contract (dedup on Line.ID+IsComplete, never assume every frame). Tier 1 (external agent, any language): subscribe to finalized lines, POST ActionRequest (speak/display/session.*); build a deterministic command then a lookup that speaks an answer back. Tier 2 (in-process Go): implement AgentHandler/Retriever/LLMClient, use CompositeHandler to shortcut LLM via IntentMatcher, show LLMClient swap = local/offline deployment mode. Each tier ends with real 'moonshine serve' flags (--transport ws,grpc, --allow-actions, reused --arch/--language/--providers) and the frozen contracts it depends on. Relates to but does not duplicate P7 (.11, README/AGENTS).

title: P5d [Track B]: Retriever interface for lookup/RAG tool (retriever.go)
description:
TRACK B. Owns internal/serve/retriever.go ONLY.

- A NoopRetriever and a simple in-memory/static Retriever for tests + demo. This is the backend the Gemini 'lookup'/'retrieve' tool (P5b) calls.

title: P5a [Track B]: AgentHandler interface + ExternalAgent (agent.go)
description:
TRACK B. Owns internal/serve/agent.go ONLY.

Build:
- type AgentHandler interface { OnFinalizedLine(ctx, moonshine.Line) []event.ActionRequest }
- ExternalAgent: no built-in logic — returns nil (subscribers own all logic via IPC). This is the default so the daemon is a pure event/action relay.
- Agent runner: consumes Hub events (or a finalized-line channel), and for each newly-finalized line (dedup on Line.ID) calls the configured AgentHandler and forwards returned ActionRequests to the Dispatcher (P2). Optional end-of-turn silence debounce so the agent acts on complete utterances.

TESTS: agent_test.go — feed fake finalized lines, assert ExternalAgent produces no actions and that a fake handler's actions reach a fake dispatcher exactly once per Line.ID.
design: Depends on P1 event types + P2 Dispatcher interface. Isolated to agent.go. GeminiAgent (P5b) and intent matcher (P5c) are separate files implementing/feeding this interface.
acceptance_criteria:
- AgentHandler interface + ExternalAgent + runner defined.
- Finalized-line dedup + forward-to-dispatcher unit tested with fakes.
- No native libs.
close_reason: Implemented AgentHandler interface, ExternalAgent, CompositeHandler, and AgentRunner with deduplication and unit tests.

title: P5c [Track B]: fast-path intent matcher (intent.go)
description:
TRACK B. Owns internal/serve/intent.go ONLY. Deterministic voice-command fast path that runs BEFORE Gemini (cheap, offline).

Build:
- IntentMatcher: regex/rule table mapping finalized utterances to event.ActionRequest (e.g. /^(stop|pause) listening$/ -> session.pause; /^say (.+)$/ -> speak{text:...}). Config-loadable rules.
- Implements AgentHandler (from P5a) so it slots into the runner; returns actions on match, nil on miss (falls through to Gemini in the composite handler).

NOTE: This is the lightweight fast path. A future richer version can use moonshine's embedding-based Intent Recognizer — see linked issue moonshine-go-tpg (Intent Recognizer bindings). Do NOT block on that; regex/rules only here.

TESTS: intent_test.go — table of utterances -> expected actions / no-match.
design: Pure Go, no native deps, no network. Implements AgentHandler. Composable with GeminiAgent via a CompositeHandler (define in agent.go P5a or here — coordinate: put CompositeHandler in agent.go to keep intent.go single-purpose).
acceptance_criteria:
- IntentMatcher implements AgentHandler; rule table + at least one deterministic command tested.
- No native libs / no network in tests.

title: P4a [Track A]: WebSocket transport (ws.go)
description:
TRACK A. Owns internal/serve/ws.go ONLY. Implements Transport over WebSocket (browser-friendly for the display-info UI).

Build:
- WSTransport implementing the P3 Transport interface. On connect: register subscriber with Hub (via injected Subscribe fn), stream published events as JSON text frames. Read inbound frames as event.ActionRequest -> push to Actions() channel.
- Handle backpressure per P1 Hub semantics (drop-oldest interim; finalized delivered once).
- Graceful close, ping/pong keepalive.
- Choose gorilla/websocket OR nhooyr.io/websocket (add to go.mod).

TESTS: ws_test.go using httptest.Server + a real client dialing it — connect, receive an event, send an action, assert it appears on Actions(). This uses net only, no native moonshine libs — acceptable for 'make test'.
design: Depends only on P3 interface + P1 Hub subscribe. Does NOT touch dispatcher/agent files. Keep a single ws.go file to avoid collision with Track B.
acceptance_criteria:
- WSTransport satisfies Transport; connect/publish/action round-trip tested via httptest.
- go.mod updated with the websocket dep.
- No native moonshine libs in tests.

title: P4b [Track A]: gRPC transport (serve.proto, grpc.go)
description:
TRACK A. Owns internal/serve/serve.proto and internal/serve/grpc.go (+ generated *.pb.go). Implements Transport over a gRPC bidirectional stream.

Build:
- serve.proto: service VoiceSidecar { rpc Stream(stream ActionRequest) returns (stream Event); } with Event (oneof transcript|display|action_result) and ActionRequest messages mirroring internal/serve/event types. Add buf/protoc codegen (protoc-gen-go, protoc-gen-go-grpc) — document the gen command in a Makefile target 'proto'.
- grpc.go: GRPCTransport implementing P3 Transport. Server-side stream publishes events; client->server stream carries ActionRequests -> Actions() channel. Register subscriber with Hub on stream open.
- Add google.golang.org/grpc + protobuf deps to go.mod.

TESTS: grpc_test.go using bufconn in-memory listener + generated client — open stream, receive an event, send an action, assert on Actions(). No native moonshine libs.
design: Depends only on P3 interface + P1 Hub subscribe/event types. Isolated to serve.proto/grpc.go/*.pb.go — no overlap with ws.go (Track A sibling) or Track B files. Coordinate ONLY on go.mod with P4a (both add deps) — expect a trivial merge there.
acceptance_criteria:

- GRPCTransport satisfies Transport; bufconn round-trip (event out, action in) tested.

title: Implement audio playback using malgo in internal/audio
description:
Add a --play flag to 'moonshine tts' that plays the synthesized audio through the default output device immediately after synthesis, in addition to (not instead of) writing the -o WAV file. Needed a genuinely cross-platform playback path since this is a CLI tool used on macOS/Linux/Windows.

Solved via the existing github.com/gen2brain/malgo dependency (already used for internal/audio/mic.go's mic capture) -- miniaudio, which malgo wraps, supports a Playback DeviceType alongside the Capture type already in use, backed by CoreAudio (macOS) / WASAPI (Windows) / ALSA+PulseAudio (Linux) under one API. No new dependency, no new cgo requirement (mic capture already required cgo).
design:
internal/audio/playback.go (new): PlayFloat32(samples []float32, sampleRate int32) error, mirroring MicCapture's device setup but with malgo.Playback + a Data callback that copies from the samples slice into miniaudio's output buffer (zero-filling once exhausted). Blocks by sleeping for the clip's real duration plus a fixed 200ms drain margin, since miniaudio's data callback only signals when audio has been queued, not when it's finished actually playing out through the device's own buffering.

cmd/moonshine/tts.go: added a --play bool flag (viper-bound like the other tts.* flags); runTTS calls audio.PlayFloat32(out.Samples, out.SampleRate) after the existing SaveWAV call when set.

docs/user-guide.md: added --play to the tts flag table and an example.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'moonshine tts --play "..."' plays synthesized audio through the default output device and still writes the WAV file to -o/default out.wav
- Cross-platform via malgo/miniaudio (CoreAudio/WASAPI/ALSA+PulseAudio) -- no per-OS special-casing needed
- Manually verified on macOS: correct wall-clock timing (clip duration + ~200ms drain + device init overhead) and no errors

title: Add live.arch config key; doctor checks both transcribe/live archs
description: 'live --arch' currently has no config-key backing at all -- runLive calls modelArchFromFlag(liveArch) directly instead of going through flagOrConfig, so unlike stt.arch (shared by setup/transcribe) there's no way to persist a preferred live arch via config.yaml/env. This was intentional as far as NOT sharing stt.arch (streaming needs a different default), but the lack of any persisted key at all looks like a gap rather than a deliberate choice. Separately, 'moonshine doctor' checkModel only checks one (language, arch) pair (stt.arch's), so it could report [OK] on the STT model check while live's actual arch model is still missing.
design:
1. cmd/moonshine/live.go: runLive switches from modelArchFromFlag(liveArch) to flagOrConfig(cmd, "arch", "live.arch", liveArch) then modelArchFromFlag on the result. Update --arch flag help text to mention config key live.arch.
2. cmd/moonshine/root.go initViper: viper.SetDefault("live.arch", "tiny-streaming").
3. cmd/moonshine/config.go: add live.arch to configKeys table; tighten stt.arch's doc string now that live has its own real key instead of just a hardcoded flag default.
4. cmd/moonshine/doctor.go: extend checkModel (or add a second check) to check live.arch's model directory too, as a distinct row (e.g. 'STT model (transcribe)' / 'STT model (live)') only when live.arch != stt.arch (avoid a redundant duplicate row when they match).
5. setup itself is explicitly NOT changed (Option A, decided) -- it still resolves only stt.arch and downloads one (language,arch) pair per invocation; no auto-download-for-live-too behavior. Docs should make this two-invocation pattern explicit rather than implied.
6. Docs: README config table gets a live.arch row; a short explicit note near the Quickstart that setup has no knowledge of live.arch and must be run once per arch used; docs/user-guide.md's live section flag table + setup/config sections updated; docs/faq.md gets a short 'why do I need to run setup twice' Q&A.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'moonshine live' honors live.arch from config.yaml/env when --arch isn't explicitly passed, defaulting to tiny-streaming
- 'moonshine config list' shows live.arch with correct value/provenance
- 'moonshine doctor' reports separate rows for the transcribe-arch and live-arch model directories when they differ (and doesn't duplicate the row when they're the same)
- setup's own behavior is unchanged (still single (language,arch) per invocation, resolves stt.arch only)
- README/user-guide/faq updated per the design notes above
close_reason: Added live.arch config key (own default tiny-streaming, threaded through live.go's runLive via flagOrConfig same as stt.arch); root.go SetDefault, config.go configKeys entry + example; doctor.go's model check split into checkModels() reporting separate 'STT model (transcribe)'/'STT model (live)' rows (only when they differ). setup intentionally left unchanged (Option A). README/user-guide/faq updated to make the two-invocation setup pattern explicit. Verified end-to-end: config set/list, doctor's dual rows, live honoring live.arch by default and --arch overriding it, and the pre-existing (unrelated) base-streaming/en manifest gap reproduced identically via 'setup --arch base-streaming' confirming live.arch threads through the same code path as stt.arch.

title: Track partial-transcript stability stats (time-to-final, revisions) in live
description: Neither 'transcribe' nor 'live' currently track how long an interim line takes to stabilize/finalize, or how many times its text was revised while still partial. transcribe has no partial state at all (single batch call, every line arrives IsComplete). live does have partial states (interim lines revised as more audio arrives) but only tracks session-level TTFT/Elapsed/PollLatency and per-line LastLatencyMs (inference time) -- nothing aggregates per-line text-change history into a stability/flicker metric. The C API already exposes the raw ingredients per line (stable ID across polls, IsNew, IsUpdated, HasTextChanged, IsComplete) -- nothing downstream (internal/session, internal/tui/live.go, cmd/moonshine/live.go) currently accumulates them across updates.
design:
New types in internal/session/session.go:
  - LineTiming{ID, TimeToFinal, PollCount, Revisions, StabilityRatio} -- StabilityRatio = 1 - Revisions/PollCount (PollCount-normalized rather than a raw revision count, so a line seen 20x with 2 revisions doesn't look as unstable as one seen 3x with 2 revisions).
  - SessionSummary{LinesFinalized, AvgTimeToFinal, MaxTimeToFinal, AvgRevisions, MaxRevisions, AvgStabilityRatio} -- aggregates LineTiming across every line finalized in the session.
  Update gains: FinalizedLines []LineTiming (non-empty only on the poll where line(s) just completed) and Summary *SessionSummary (set only on the final Done update).

  Tracking logic lives in Live.Run's poll closure (session.go ~line 80-94): maintain an internal map[uint64]*lineProgress{firstSeen time.Time, pollCount, revisions int, lastText string, done bool} keyed by Line.ID. On each poll, for every line in the transcript: first sighting starts the clock (skip lines with empty text); while !IsComplete, increment pollCount every sighting and revisions when text changed since last sighting; the poll where IsComplete first becomes true computes TimeToFinal=time.Since(firstSeen), appends to Live's running []LineTiming (source for the end-of-session SessionSummary), and to this poll's Update.FinalizedLines. Attach the computed SessionSummary to the final Done update (session.go line ~101, after the last poll(moonshine.FlagForceUpdate) call).

  Note: Live.send already drops updates under backpressure (channel buffer 8, 'best effort... next tick carries a superset'). A dropped update could lose a FinalizedLines notification for live display, same accepted tradeoff the code already has for transcript updates -- doesn't affect SessionSummary since that's accumulated inside Live regardless of whether an Update was actually sent.

  Display:
  - TUI (internal/tui/live.go): extend statsLine()/liveModel to show the most recent FinalizedLines entry inline with the existing ttft/elapsed/last_poll footer (e.g. 'last_line: ttf=640ms revisions=2'), updated whenever one arrives. On Done, extend the footer with the full SessionSummary.
  - --no-tui plain mode (cmd/moonshine/live.go runLivePlain): the existing final stats: line (stderr) always gets SessionSummary fields added (low noise, one line). Per-line finalize notes are gated behind a new opt-in bool flag --line-stats (default false, stderr), to avoid changing today's --no-tui output format for anyone scripting/logging against it.

  Explicitly out of scope: live's --json mode doesn't exist for the transcript stream at all today (jsonOutput() there only suppresses status text) -- not building that as part of this. HaveSpeakersChanged firing on already-complete lines (diarization retroactively revising speaker assignments under --identify-speakers) is a related but distinct kind of post-finalization 'flicker' -- not counted in Revisions, which is pre-finalization text changes only.
acceptance_criteria:
- internal/session/session.go: LineTiming and SessionSummary types added; Update gains FinalizedLines []LineTiming and Summary *SessionSummary
- Live.Run tracks per-line firstSeen/pollCount/revisions via Line.ID across polls, computing TimeToFinal/StabilityRatio when a line finalizes
- SessionSummary is computed and attached to the final Done update, aggregating every finalized line in the session
- TUI (internal/tui/live.go) shows the latest per-line finalize stat in its stats footer during the session, and the full SessionSummary once Done
- --no-tui plain mode always prints the SessionSummary in its final stats: line; per-line finalize notes only print when a new --line-stats flag is passed
- Verified against a real multi-utterance live/streaming run (or the streaming smoke test) that TimeToFinal/Revisions/StabilityRatio are populated and plausible
- docs/user-guide.md's live section documents the new --line-stats flag and the summary stats
close_reason: Added LineTiming/SessionSummary to internal/session (Live.trackLines/summarize), threading FinalizedLines/Summary through Update. TUI shows last_line + summary in its stats footer; --no-tui gets an opt-in --line-stats flag for per-line stderr notes plus an always-on summary line. Unit-tested the tracking/aggregation logic directly (internal/session/session_test.go: revision counting, first-sighting finalization, empty-text skip, multi-line-per-poll, summarize aggregation) since live's mic capture can't be exercised headlessly in this sandbox (same limitation already documented for other live work). docs/user-guide.md updated.

title: Add 'moonshine doctor' subcommand for prerequisite/health checks
description: All prereq/troubleshooting knowledge today is passive documentation (README 'Prerequisites' section + docs/user-guide.md 'Troubleshooting'), reactive by design -- a user only learns something's missing when a command fails with an error string mid-run. There's no proactive check command. Add 'moonshine doctor' to consolidate this into one command, with OS-aware guidance (brew vs apt-style fix commands, etc.).
design:
New cmd/moonshine/doctor.go (cobra, --json-capable like other commands). Two tiers of checks:

1. Build-time prereqs (relevant before libmoonshine exists): cmake present + version, a C++20-capable compiler, git-lfs installed, a C toolchain for live's cgo build. Mirror scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh's own implicit requirements.
2. Runtime prereqs: --lib-dir/MOONSHINE_LIB_DIR resolves and the library actually dlopens (reuse loadLibrary()), model dir has files for the configured (stt.language, stt.arch) pair (reuse sttModelDir), best-effort GCS ADC discoverability (e.g. GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS set, or gcloud auth application-default print-access-token succeeds if gcloud is present) for users likely to hit gs:// input, and (best-effort) tts.g2p_root/voice asset sanity if configured.

Output: a pass/warn/fail table per check (style consistent with 'moonshine config list'), with OS-specific fix commands. Once this lands, README's Prerequisites section and docs/user-guide.md's Troubleshooting section should be trimmed to point at 'moonshine doctor' instead of duplicating raw diagnostic steps.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'moonshine doctor' runs on macOS and Linux, checking both build-time and runtime prerequisites listed above
- --json output available for scripting
- clear pass/warn/fail per check with actionable, OS-specific fix suggestions
- README.md Prerequisites + docs/user-guide.md Troubleshooting updated to reference 'moonshine doctor' rather than duplicating the same diagnostic steps
close_reason: Added 'moonshine doctor' subcommand: build-time checks (cmake, C++ compiler, git-lfs, Go toolchain, CGO_ENABLED) and runtime checks (libmoonshine load, STT model presence for --language/--arch, GCS ADC best-effort, tts.g2p_root best-effort), table + --json output, non-zero exit on FAIL. README/user-guide/faq updated to reference it.

title: Add --identify-speakers/--word-timestamps flags to transcribe/live
description: internal/moonshine/stt.go's Line struct already models SpeakerSpan, HaveSpeakersChanged, and Word (word_timestamps) -- moonshine.LoadTranscriber accepts arbitrary Option{Name,Value} pairs that would enable them (identify_speakers, word_timestamps, diarization_cluster_window_sec, diarization_cluster_cadence, diarization_analyze_cadence per moonshine-c-api.h), but 'moonshine transcribe'/'moonshine live' expose no flags to turn these on. Users currently cannot get speaker/word-level output from the CLI even though the plumbing to parse it already exists (see docs/faq.md's timestamps section, which already flags this gap).
design: Add --identify-speakers, --word-timestamps (bool flags) to transcribe.go and live.go, threading them through as moonshine.Option{Name:...} to loadTranscriberFor/tr.Transcribe (or the stream constructor for live). Consider also exposing diarization tuning flags (diarization_cluster_window_sec, diarization_cluster_cadence, diarization_analyze_cadence) at least on 'live' where streaming diarization matters most. --json output already has the speaker_spans/words fields (omitempty) -- just needs them populated. Human-readable output should render speaker labels/word timings when enabled.
acceptance_criteria:
- transcribe/live gain flags to enable identify_speakers and word_timestamps
- --json output populates speaker_spans/words accordingly when enabled (verify against a multi-speaker test clip if available)
- human-readable (non-JSON) output renders speaker labels and/or word timing when the flags are set
- README/docs updated (docs/faq.md's existing word_timestamps note can point at the new flag instead of describing it as unexposed)
close_reason: Added --identify-speakers/--word-timestamps to transcribe and live (plus diarization tuning flags on live), threaded through as transcriber-creation moonshine.Option pairs; JSON output already had the fields, added human-readable rendering (speaker labels, word timing summary) to transcribe/live plain output and the live TUI. Verified end-to-end against real audio (beckett.wav, two_cities_16k.wav).

No Go code changes -- CLI already supports --json, gs:// input, -o output.
design: Directory: skills/moonshine-transcribe/ (name matches agentskills.io dir-name-must-match-frontmatter rule; project-prefixed to avoid collisions when skill dirs get aggregated across repos). SKILL.md stays lean (frontmatter + short workflow: check setup/model+lib present -> run 'moonshine transcribe --json <path|gs://...>' -> parse lines/stats -> surface errors). Move exhaustive flag/env-var/error reference into skills/moonshine-transcribe/references/flags.md so SKILL.md stays under ~500 lines per spec guidance.

title: Distill serve-sidecar.md into a developer pattern doc, then retire it
description: serve-sidecar.md is the multi-agent BUILD-coordination contract (file-ownership map, bd task graph) for epic 6nb, now that the build is merged. Distill its durable, developer-facing bits (architecture, IPC/wire contract, invariants) into a concise developer 'serve pattern/overview' doc, and drop the build-time-only content (ownership map, task graph). Then retire/redirect serve-sidecar.md so the public doc set is developer-facing.
design: New docs/serve-pattern.md (concise) sourced from serve-sidecar.md architecture + IPC sections; remove file-ownership + dependency-graph sections. Repoint references (docs/README, quickstart, hosting.md) to the new doc. Retire serve-sidecar.md (or reduce to a historical build-record note).

As the AudioSource/TTS-bytes/session-manager/container tasks land, update the 'What you have to build' section to reflect what's now built vs pending, and add concrete run/deploy commands.

title: Swappable Transcriber backend (local-default, optional cloud STT) — hybrid future option
description: Lesson from the online STT landscape: hybrid is the honest end-state. Define a Transcriber backend interface so serve can use local Moonshine by default but optionally route to a cloud STT (e.g. for max-accuracy or exotic-language jobs), mirroring the swappable LLMClient pattern. MUST preserve the air-gapped guarantee: cloud STT is opt-in, OFF by default, and hard-disabled in air-gapped mode (raw audio must not leave the box unless explicitly allowed). Deliberate FUTURE option, not committed — this task is design + scoping first.
design: Architectural. Interface abstraction over the current internal/moonshine Transcriber; local impl stays default. Cloud backend is a separate opt-in impl gated by config, disabled under an air-gapped/no-egress flag. Contrast with the deployment-mode framing in opportunities.md §2: this adds a swappable EARS to match the swappable BRAIN, without weakening the 'audio dies at the mic' default. Keep native-free tests via a fake Transcriber.
acceptance_criteria: Design doc / interface proposal for a swappable Transcriber; local Moonshine remains the default; cloud backend (if implemented) is opt-in, off by default, and provably disabled in air-gapped mode; air-gapped guarantee documented and tested.

title: Domain vocabulary / keyterm boosting for regulated verticals
description: Lesson from Deepgram keyterm boosting + ElevenLabs: regulated verticals (medical drug names, legal case numbers, financial tickers) need domain-vocabulary biasing at recognition time. Investigate and, if upstream support lands, expose a keyterm/keyword-boost option on the transcriber. Complements (does not replace) the RAG story in opportunities.md.
design: OPEN QUESTION / genuine upstream caveat: moonshine-c-api.h's recognized-options list (log_ort_run, ort_providers, identify_speakers, diarization_*, spelling_model_path) does NOT include keyterm/keyword/vocab/hotword/boost. tokenizer.bin/vocab is the model's token mapping, not runtime biasing. This is NOT currently possible via the C API. FIRST STEP: confirm with upstream libmoonshine whether recognition-time vocabulary biasing is on their roadmap; do not implement a moonshine-go shim that pretends to boost. If unsupported, consider a post-hoc correction/spelling-fusion approach (spelling_model_path exists) as a partial alternative.
acceptance_criteria: Either: (a) upstream keyterm-boost support confirmed and exposed as a transcriber option with tests; or (b) documented finding that it is unsupported upstream, with the spelling-fusion alternative evaluated. No fake 'boosting' that doesn't actually bias recognition.

title: Adopt prebuilt Linux release asset once onnxruntime bundling + RPATH fix lands upstream
description:
moonshine-ai/moonshine now publishes prebuilt moonshine-voice-linux-x86_64.tar.gz assets on GitHub Releases (confirmed present as of v0.0.69, uploaded 2026-07-20), which would let moonshine-go skip building libmoonshine from source entirely on Linux -- no C++ toolchain, no git-lfs pull of the moonshine core checkout, just a curl+tar of a ~14MB archive.

NOT YET ACTIONABLE: verified via scripts/check-release-asset.sh that the current v0.0.69 Linux asset has two real gaps that make it unusable as-is for purego's dlopen-based loading:
1. libmoonshine.so dynamically depends on libonnxruntime.so.1 (confirmed via strings/otool dependency scan), but the archive does not include libonnxruntime.so.1 -- dlopen would fail to resolve it on any machine that doesn't already have it installed system-wide.
2. libmoonshine.so's runtime search path for that dependency is a hardcoded absolute path on the upstream maintainer's own build machine (/home/pete/moonshine-git/core/third-party/onnxruntime/lib/linux/x86_64), not a portable relative path (RPATH ) -- meaning even if libonnxruntime were bundled next to it in the same directory, the loader wouldn't find it there.

Reported upstream; maintainer (Pete Warden) confirmed both are 'straightforward improvements' and is investigating the necessary CI changes (separately from #1/macOS-Windows shared-library support, which has no ETA -- see the sibling issue for that).
design:
Once fixed upstream (verify with 'scripts/check-release-asset.sh linux-x86_64 <tag>' -- should report PASS on both the onnxruntime-bundled and portable-RPATH checks), the actual adoption work here is:

1. Decide where this fits relative to scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh (source-build path) -- likely a new script (e.g. scripts/fetch-libmoonshine.sh or a --prebuilt flag on the existing one) that downloads and extracts moonshine-voice-linux-x86_64.tar.gz for a pinned release tag into .moonshine/lib/, matching the same output layout build-libmoonshine.sh already produces (so internal/moonshine.ResolveLibPath doesn't need to change).
2. Establish the actual version pin: which moonshine-ai/moonshine release tag does a given moonshine-go commit/release trust for prebuilt assets? This should be an explicit, deliberately-bumped value (not 'whatever's latest at build time') -- e.g. a constant or a MOONSHINE_RELEASE_TAG file at the repo root, checked by both this fetch script and CI. Bump it only when actually verified compatible (HeaderVersion in internal/moonshine/lib.go, currently 20000, is the runtime compatibility floor this pin must not violate -- verify moonshine_get_version() from the fetched release still satisfies it).
3. This unblocks a real GH Actions release/build pipeline for Linux specifically (build+package the moonshine-go CLI binary + fetched libmoonshine.so + libonnxruntime.so into a downloadable artifact) without touching moonshine-ai's git-lfs bandwidth at all -- a GitHub Release download doesn't consume LFS quota the way a git clone of the LFS-heavy core checkout does.
4. macOS and Windows remain source-build-only (or manual) until the sibling issue (shared library support) is resolved upstream -- don't block this on those.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'scripts/check-release-asset.sh linux-x86_64 <pinned-tag>' passes all checks (shared lib present, onnxruntime bundled, portable rpath)
- A documented, deliberately-bumped version pin exists recording which moonshine-ai/moonshine release tag this repo trusts for prebuilt Linux assets
- A fetch mechanism exists that stages the downloaded libmoonshine.so + libonnxruntime.so into the same .moonshine/lib/ layout scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh already produces, verified via 'make smoke' passing against the fetched (not locally-built) library
close_reason: Created MOONSHINE_RELEASE_TAG pinned to v0.0.71, scripts/fetch-libmoonshine.sh, and make fetchlib Makefile target

title: Bind grapheme-to-phonemizer G2P API + moonshine_phonemes_to_speech; add 'tts --ipa'
description:
Two related, currently-fully-unbound pieces of moonshine's TTS C API, discovered together while reviewing an upstream pull (moonshine core commit 6a0439d 'Added phoneme to speech support in TTS'):

1. moonshine_phonemes_to_speech (NEW as of that commit): synthesizes speech directly from an IPA phoneme string, skipping grapheme-to-phoneme (G2P) conversion. Same synthesizer handle/options ('speed' etc.) as moonshine_text_to_speech. This is the 'second half' of a G2P-inspect-and-edit workflow -- lets a caller fix mispronunciation (e.g. proper nouns) by hand-editing IPA between G2P and vocoding.
2. moonshine_create_grapheme_to_phonemizer_from_memory / moonshine_text_to_phonemes / moonshine_free_grapheme_to_phonemizer (PRE-EXISTING, was already unbound before this pull -- not new, just discovered to be a gap while investigating #1): the G2P step itself, producing the IPA string moonshine_phonemes_to_speech (or the internal G2P moonshine_text_to_speech already does automatically) consumes.

Currently internal/moonshine has ZERO references to phonemes, IPA, text_to_phonemes, or grapheme_to_phonemizer anywhere -- confirmed via search. Binding only moonshine_phonemes_to_speech without also binding the G2P step would only support callers who hand-author raw IPA themselves; binding both gives the full inspect-and-edit workflow moonshine's own docs describe for this feature (see moonshine core's micro/neural-tts docs for the 'Reading, Pennsylvania' example of exactly this use case: default G2P misreads a place name, SynthesizeIpa/phonemes_to_speech with hand-corrected IPA fixes it).

MOONSHINE_HEADER_VERSION is unchanged (still 20000) by the commit that added this -- no version bump, no compatibility concern, just new unbound API surface.
design:
internal/moonshine/g2p.go (new file): ctypes + Go wrappers for the G2P chain. Confirmed both moonshine_create_grapheme_to_phonemizer_from_files AND ..._from_memory exist (mirror the transcriber/synthesizer pattern of supporting both -- LoadTranscriber uses from_files, NewSynthesizer uses from_files too via moonshine_create_tts_synthesizer_from_files with filenames=nil; prefer from_files for the initial binding, consistent with the rest of this package, add from_memory later only if a real need arises), moonshine_text_to_phonemes, and moonshine_free_grapheme_to_phonemizer. Mirror the pattern in tts.go (Synthesizer type) -- a Phonemizer type wrapping the handle with Close()/finalizer, a TextToPhonemes(text string) (string, error) method.

internal/moonshine/tts.go: add PhonemesToSpeech(phonemes string, opts ...Option) (Audio, error) method on the existing Synthesizer type (or a standalone function taking a synthesizer handle) wrapping moonshine_phonemes_to_speech -- same option/audio-decoding pattern as the existing Synthesize method.

cmd/moonshine/tts.go: add a --ipa flag (mutually exclusive with the positional text argument, similar to how neural-tts's own host CLI tts_cli does '--ipa' vs a positional text arg per the Micro investigation) that calls PhonemesToSpeech directly, skipping G2P. Consider also exposing text-to-phonemes standalone (e.g. 'moonshine tts --show-phonemes' or a separate small subcommand) so users can see default G2P output before deciding whether to hand-edit it -- needed for the edit workflow to be practically usable, not just phonemes-to-speech in isolation.

Docs: docs/user-guide.md's tts section gets an IPA override subsection with the Reading/Pennsylvania-style example; README docs list unaffected.
acceptance_criteria:
- internal/moonshine has ctypes + wrappers for the full G2P chain (NewPhonemizer/TextToPhonemes/Close) plus Synthesizer.PhonemesToSpeech -- DONE
- 'moonshine tts --ipa "..."' synthesizes audio directly from hand-written IPA, verified against kokoro_af_heart -- DONE
- 'moonshine tts --show-phonemes "..."' prints default G2P output (chose a flag, not a subcommand) -- DONE
- docs/user-guide.md documents the IPA override workflow with a real, verified mispronunciation-fixing example ("Reading, Pennsylvania": G2P's ɹˈidɪŋ -> hand-corrected ˈɹɛdɪŋ) -- DONE
- Bonus: mutual-exclusivity (--ipa + <text>) and missing-arg (--show-phonemes with no text) validation added and verified
- Full moonshinesmoke suite still passes

title: Add Intent Recognizer bindings
description: moonshine-c-api.h's INTENT RECOGNIZER section (moonshine_create_intent_recognizer, moonshine_register_intent/unregister_intent, moonshine_get_closest_intents, moonshine_calculate_intent_embedding, moonshine_get_intent_dependencies for its embedding-model manifest, e.g. embeddinggemma-300m) has no Go binding at all in internal/moonshine -- not in ctypes.go, lib.go, or any wrapper type. This is a wholly new subsystem (embedding-based intent matching against registered canonical phrases) with zero moonshine-go coverage. (Split out from the original combined issue; the diarization/word-timestamps flags half is now tracked separately as moonshine-go-f4a, P2, since that only needs new CLI flags over existing plumbing -- this issue is a much bigger net-new binding + CLI surface.)
design: New internal/moonshine/intent.go with ctypes for moonshine_intent_match_t + the create/free/register/unregister/get_closest_intents/calculate_intent_embedding/free_intent_embedding/get_intent_dependencies functions (mirror the pattern in stt.go/download.go). A corresponding download path (uses moonshine_get_intent_dependencies, same DependencyManifest shape as STT) -- likely 'moonshine setup --intent-model ...' or a separate subcommand. A new CLI surface (e.g. 'moonshine intent register/match' or similar) -- needs its own UX scoping, distinct from transcribe/tts/live/setup/config.
acceptance_criteria: - internal/moonshine has a binding layer for the Intent Recognizer C API (create/free/register/unregister/get_closest_intents/calculate_intent_embedding/similarity/get_intent_dependencies)\n- some CLI surface exists to register intents and match an utterance against them, with --json output\n- README/docs updated to describe the new capability and its config keys/flags

title: Add 'moonshine mcp' subcommand exposing transcribe via MCP
description: Add an MCP server subcommand (moonshine mcp) using github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk, exposing a 'transcribe' tool over stdio for MCP-only hosts (Claude Desktop, IDE MCP integrations) that have no general shell/bash tool and thus cannot use the CLI directly. v1 scope is transcribe only -- tts/live are out of scope (live is a streaming mic session that doesn't map cleanly to a single request/response tool call).
design:
- New file cmd/moonshine/mcp.go (or internal/mcpserver package) wiring mcp.NewServer + mcp.AddTool for a 'transcribe' tool mirroring transcribeCmd's inputs (input path/gs:// URI, language, arch, providers) and transcribeResult's output shape (lines + stats), using Go struct tags for JSON schema per go-sdk conventions.
- loadLibrary() must happen lazily/once (sync.Once) on first tool call, not per-call like the CLI does.
- Add a transcriber cache keyed by (language, arch, providers) so repeated tool calls reuse an already-loaded model instead of reloading ONNX weights every call -- this is the main perf win over just shelling out to the CLI repeatedly from an MCP client.
- GCS input (gs://) reuses internal/gcsfetch; document that ADC must be valid in whatever environment launches the MCP server subprocess (host configs often strip shell env/gcloud state).
- Run via 'moonshine mcp' (stdio transport), added to rootCmd similar to other subcommands.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'moonshine mcp' subcommand starts an MCP stdio server
- 'transcribe' tool callable via mcp go-sdk test client (see go-sdk README client example) round-trips a local wav file to lines+stats matching CLI's --json output shape
- gs:// input works
- second call with same language/arch does not reload the model (verify via timing or a load-count counter in tests)
- README/docs mention the new subcommand and required env vars for MCP host configs

title: Track upstream shared library (dylib/dll) support for macOS/Windows releases
description:
Watch-only, no ETA -- this is explicitly NOT scoped/actionable work yet, just a placeholder so the open question doesn't get lost.

moonshine-ai/moonshine's prebuilt release assets (moonshine-voice-macos-arm64.tar.gz, moonshine-voice-windows-x86_64.tar.gz) currently only ship STATIC libraries (libmoonshine.a on macOS, moonshine.lib on Windows), confirmed via scripts/check-release-asset.sh against v0.0.69. purego (this project's FFI mechanism) requires a dynamically loadable shared library (dlopen/.dylib on macOS, LoadLibrary/.dll on Windows) -- a static archive can't be dlopen'd, so these two platforms' prebuilt assets are not usable for moonshine-go regardless of any onnxruntime-bundling/RPATH fix (that's the sibling Linux issue, moonshine-go-glg, which IS on a 'straightforward' track).

Reported upstream; maintainer (Pete Warden) response: 'investigating how to update the CI testing to cover both static and dynamic libraries' -- i.e. this requires him to stand up a whole second build+test matrix (dual static/shared artifacts), not just flip a CMake flag, since MOONSHINE_BUILD_SHARED=ON changes how the existing Swift/iOS (macOS) and C++ example app (Windows) consumers link against it, and he doesn't yet have CI coverage to catch regressions in that path.

Worth noting for prioritization: macOS is the platform this project's own dev machine and CI would benefit from most (this repo's local dev loop runs on darwin/arm64), but it's on the slower/uncertain track here, unlike Linux.
design:
No design yet -- there's nothing to build until upstream ships a shared macOS/Windows artifact. When it does:
1. Re-run scripts/check-release-asset.sh macos-arm64/windows-x86_64 <tag> to verify it's actually usable (shared lib + bundled onnxruntime + portable search path, same three checks as the Linux issue).
2. If it passes, this becomes essentially the same adoption work as moonshine-go-glg, extended to the other two platforms -- extend whatever fetch mechanism gets built there rather than designing a separate one.
3. Until then, macOS/Windows continue to require scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh (source build from a local moonshine checkout) as the only path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Re-check periodically (or when Pete announces movement) via 'scripts/check-release-asset.sh macos-arm64 <tag>' and 'scripts/check-release-asset.sh windows-x86_64 <tag>'
- Once either passes, convert this into real scoped work (or fold into moonshine-go-glg's fetch mechanism) rather than leaving it as a watch-only placeholder
